entry.title: Re: How to manage documents in SAP Document AI
entry.content:
The REST APIs are /document-information-extraction/v1, the Odata APIs are /document-ai/v1. In the swagger tutorial and Build Process Automation, the REST APIs are used with a default retention period of 7 days. The retention period is configured via a separate UI / API, not via the document upload call. See: https://help.sap.com/docs/document-ai/sap-document-ai/configuration-keys

I'm not an expert for Build Process Automation, therefore, I cannot answer question 3.

entry.title: Live Sessions for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Fundamentals in June 2026
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Fundamentals Live Session Series

We’re excited to announce a full month of expert‑led live sessions designed to help you deepen your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition knowledge, sharpen your implementation skills, and prepare for certification. Whether you're just getting started or already on your learning journey, these sessions offer valuable insights, best practices, and hands‑on guidance directly from SAP instructors.

Below you’ll find the full schedule for June 2026—including titles, dates, times, and registration links. Mark your calendar and secure your spot!

Please note that you need a valid SAP Learning Hub subscription to enroll in the sessions.

🕒17:00–18:00 CEST

entry.title: What business trend is currently having the biggest impact on your SAP projects?
entry.content: I feel like there’s always a new “big thing” everyone is talking about, lately it has been AI, clean core, cloud, UX… but I’m not sure all of them actually change what we do day to day. I wanted to ask if you really see a difference in your projects with all these trends. I mean, is there something that really made a difference?  Something that actually changed how you work, not just something that sounds good in presentations.

entry.title: Re: Restrict VPN access at the user level so that the use...
entry.content:
Dear @Manjusha2,

In Public Cloud the standard setup does not provide an S/4 or CBC setting to restrict a user so that they can log in only through one assigned VPN. SAP documents that access to the Public Cloud tenant is controlled by SAP Cloud Identity Services Identity Authentication and that Identity Authentication acts as the identity provider for Public Cloud logon. Therefore this requirement must be enforced at the identity layer, not in S/4 business role authorization itself

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/b60dd89024994573873a231502d4ddd4.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/421a275568014cbfb7c74824588b1401.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/33565618640340cda644d11d5774864c.html

If the business requirement is to allow access only when the user comes through the assigned VPN the standard approach is to configure Conditional Authentication in SAP Cloud Identity Services for the Cloud application. SAP documents that authentication rules can be defined based on criteria such as user group and IP range in CIDR notation and SAP also documents group management and assignment of users to groups in Identity Authentication. In practice this means the user is assigned to a dedicated IAS group and access is allowed only when the login comes from the public IP range of the required VPN. This works reliably only if the assigned VPN has its own fixed public egress IP range. Because the standard IAS condition is based on user group and IP range, standard SAP can distinguish source networks only when the VPNs expose different public IPs. If multiple VPNs share the same public IP range the standard solution can enforce access only from the corporate VPN/network but not reliably one specific VPN per specific user

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/user-groups

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/assign-groups-to-user

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-conditional-authentication-for-application

If your landscape uses a corporate identity provider behind IAS, the same restriction can also be enforced there but from the Public Cloud perspective it remains an identity-provider control rather than an S/4 authorization setting. SAP documents that every Public Cloud instance uses Identity Authentication and that you can enable authentication by a corporate identity provider integrated with it

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/f1bfb15e6e1044e286767c56b566f9a3.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/33565618640340cda644d11d5774864c.html

Summary:

  • Use IAS Conditional Authentication, not S/4 authorization to restrict user logon by VPN

  • The standard design is user group + VPN public IP range

  • This supports per-user/per-VPN enforcement only when each VPN has a distinct fixed public egress IP

  • If VPNs share the same public IP, standard SAP can enforce VPN-only access but not one specific VPN per specific user

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP AI Core: Deployment and Configuration Errors and How to Fix Them (Part 2 of 2)
entry.content:
SAP COMMUNITY  |  TROUBLESHOOTING  |  SAP AI CORE  |  PART 2 OF 2

SAP AI Core: Deployment and Configuration Errors and How to Fix Them

Every error between a working bearer token and a working model response — 404 on inference calls, two AI Core instance confusion, orchestration vs foundation-models mismatch, resource group provisioning failures, and 409 on delete. Part 2 of 2.

By Deepa Dorairaj   |  SAP AI Solution Architect  |  SAP AI Core Troubleshooting Series  |  Published 2026

TL;DR: Part 1 covered getting your bearer token working. This part covers everything after that — why your inference call returns 404 even though the deployment shows RUNNING, how two AI Core instances cause invisible failures, why the orchestration scenario requires a different request format, and what to do when resource group creation fails with 500 or gets stuck in Provisioning. These errors are harder to diagnose because the symptoms are misleading and the causes are architectural, not syntactic.

Starting Point for This Article

This article assumes Part 1 is complete — you have a working bearer token from a successful OAuth call. The errors here all occur after authentication is working. If you're still hitting 401 or token issues, start with Part 1.

The six errors in this article follow the same sequence most practitioners hit: successfully authenticated, deployment appears to exist, but inference calls fail. Each error reveals a layer of the AI Core architecture that isn't obvious from the documentation.

ERROR 7 OF 12  
ERROR 7    404 on inference call — deployment exists in Launchpad    {"error":{"code":"404"}} 
What You See
{"error":{"code":"404","message":"Resource not found"}}

This happens even though you can see the deployment in AI Launchpad with status RUNNING.

What Causes It

Three common causes — all produce the same 404:

  • The deployment belongs to a different AI Core instance than the one your service key authenticates against (covered in detail in Error 😎
  • The token expired between the deployments list call and the inference call — tokens are valid for 3600 seconds (1 hour)
  • The AI-Resource-Group header is missing or pointing to the wrong resource group
The Fix

First — confirm the deployment ID matches exactly. Get a fresh list with a fresh token:

curl -X GET "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/lm/deployments" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_FRESH_TOKEN" ^
  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default"

Find your deployment in the response. Confirm the id field matches the ID in your inference call URL exactly. Then run the inference call immediately with the same fresh token:

curl -X POST "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/inference/deployments/YOUR_DEPLOYMENT_ID/chat/completions" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_FRESH_TOKEN" ^
  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" ^
  -d "{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}],"max_tokens":100}"

If your deployments list returns the ID but the inference call still returns 404 — the deployment belongs to a different AI Core instance. This is Error 8.

ERROR 8 OF 12  

ERROR 8    Two AI Core instances — wrong service key    404 on RUNNING deployment 
What You See

Your deployment shows RUNNING in AI Launchpad. Your token call succeeds. Your deployments list curl returns results. But the inference call returns 404 — consistently, with every fresh token.

What Causes It

Many SAP BTP accounts have multiple AI Core service instances — created at different times for different teams or projects. AI Launchpad can connect to multiple instances and lets you switch between them using the workspace selector in the header.

The critical issue: your service key belongs to one AI Core instance. Your deployment was created using a different AI Core instance in Launchpad. Your token authenticates successfully against your instance — but that instance has no record of the deployment that lives in the other instance. The result is a 404 that looks identical to a missing deployment.

How to Diagnose It

Three checks that identify this issue in under two minutes:

  1. Check your service key — the AI_API_URL field identifies which instance your credentials belong to
  2. In AI Launchpad — check the workspace header. It shows which AI Core connection is currently active. Note the name.
  3. Check the resource group labels — in AI Launchpad → Resource Groups → edit the default group → look at the instance_id label. This shows which AI Core instance owns the resource group.

If the instance_id in the resource group labels does not match the instance your service key belongs to — you have the two-instance problem.

The Fix

Two options depending on your access:

  • Option 1 (fastest): Get a service key for the AI Core instance that owns the resource group containing your deployment. BTP Cockpit → Services → Instances → find the correct instance → Service Keys → Create. Use the new credentials for token generation and all API calls.
  • Option 2 (self-contained): Create a new resource group in your own AI Core instance, create a new configuration and deployment there, and use your existing service key throughout. This requires your service plan to support additional resource group creation — see Error 9 if this fails.

The instance_id label in the resource group is the fastest way to identify this issue. It directly tells you which AI Core instance the resource group belongs to — compare it against the instance identifier in your service key URL.

ERROR 9 OF 12  
ERROR 9    Resource group stuck in Provisioning    500 on creation / status never changes 
What You See

You create a new resource group via AI Launchpad or curl PUT. The creation returns a 500 error or appears to succeed but the status shows Provisioning and never changes — even after 10+ minutes.

What Causes It

Two common causes:

  • Your AI Core service plan does not support creating additional resource groups. Some SAP internal plans and certain BTP subscription tiers restrict resource group creation to the default group only.
  • The AI Core instance itself has an underlying provisioning issue — a platform-level problem that requires SAP support to resolve.
The Fix

Step 1 — Wait 5 minutes before concluding provisioning has failed. New resource groups typically provision in under 5 minutes. A 10+ minute wait with no status change indicates a failure.

Step 2 — Verify your service plan supports additional resource groups. Check BTP Cockpit → Services → Instances → your AI Core instance → plan details. If the plan explicitly restricts resource groups, creating new ones is not possible without upgrading.

Step 3 — If the resource group is stuck, delete it and recreate via curl (more reliable than the UI for initial creation):

# Delete stuck resource group
curl -X DELETE "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/admin/resourceGroups/YOUR_GROUP_ID" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"
 
# Recreate via curl
curl -X PUT "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/admin/resourceGroups" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" ^
  -d "{"resourceGroupId": "your-new-group"}"

Step 4 — Check status after 3 minutes:

curl -X GET "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/admin/resourceGroups/your-new-group" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"

If status remains Provisioning after recreation — raise a support ticket with component CA-ML-AIP, include your instance ID and the timestamp of the failed creation attempt.

ERROR 10 OF 12  
ERROR 10    409 on resource group delete    {"error":{"code":"409"}} 
What You See
{"error":{"code":"409","message":"Conflict"}}

This occurs when attempting to delete a resource group that is still in Provisioning state.

What Causes It

HTTP 409 Conflict means the resource is in a state that doesn't allow the requested operation. A resource group in Provisioning state is actively being worked on by the platform — deleting it while provisioning is in progress is not permitted.

The Fix

Wait for the provisioning process to either complete (status: PROVISIONED) or fail (status: ERROR) before attempting to delete. If the status has been Provisioning for over 15 minutes without changing, the platform may be unresponsive — in this case wait 30 minutes before retrying the delete.

If the delete still fails after the status shows ERROR, use the curl DELETE command rather than the UI — the API is more reliable for deleting failed resource groups:

curl -X DELETE "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/admin/resourceGroups/YOUR_GROUP_ID" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"
ERROR 11 OF 12  
ERROR 11    Orchestration vs foundation-models scenario mismatch    Empty response or wrong endpoint format 
What You See

Your deployments list returns a deployment with status RUNNING, but when you call the inference endpoint using the standard chat/completions format, you get an empty response or a format error.

What Causes It

SAP AI Core has two distinct deployment scenarios — foundation-models and orchestration. They use different inference endpoint formats and different request body structures.

  • foundation-models deployments use /chat/completions with a standard OpenAI-compatible request body including a model field
  • orchestration deployments use /completion with an orchestration_config wrapper structure — a completely different format

If your deployment was created under the orchestration scenario (check the scenarioId field in the deployments list response) but you're calling it with the foundation-models format, the request either fails silently or returns a format error.

How to Check Your Scenario

In your deployments list response, find the scenarioId field for your deployment:

{"id":"d01e9590...","scenarioId":"orchestration",...}  ← orchestration deployment
{"id":"df600c1a...","scenarioId":"foundation-models",...}  ← foundation-models deployment
The Fix — foundation-models deployment (standard format)
curl -X POST "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/inference/deployments/YOUR_ID/chat/completions" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" ^
  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" ^
  -d "{"model":"anthropic--claude-3-5-sonnet","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}],"max_tokens":100}"
The Fix — orchestration deployment (orchestration format)
curl -X POST "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/inference/deployments/YOUR_ID/completion" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" ^
  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" ^
  -d "{"orchestration_config":{"module_configurations":{"llm_module_config":{"model_name":"anthropic--claude-3-5-sonnet","model_params":{"max_tokens":500}}}},"messages_history":[],"template":{"template":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}]}}"

For most use cases — direct model access for Python applications and RAG pipelines — the foundation-models scenario is the correct choice. Orchestration is for SAP's managed prompt orchestration service with additional capabilities like content filtering and grounding. If you want direct foundation model access, create your configuration with scenarioId: foundation-models.

ERROR 12 OF 12  
ERROR 12    AI Launchpad showing wrong instance    Configurations visible but inference fails 
What You See

You create a configuration and deployment in AI Launchpad and can see it listed. But when you use curl with your service key credentials, the deployment is either not found in the list or returns 404 on inference.

What Causes It

AI Launchpad is a separate BTP subscription that can connect to multiple AI Core instances. The workspace selector in the Launchpad header controls which instance you're viewing and managing. If you have subscriptions to AI Launchpad in multiple BTP subaccounts, each may open to a different AI Core connection by default.

The result: you create a deployment while connected to instance A in Launchpad, but your service key belongs to instance B. The Launchpad URL in the browser contains the subaccount subdomain — this is the clearest indicator of which Launchpad instance you're using.

How to Diagnose It

Check the browser URL when you're in AI Launchpad. The subdomain in the URL identifies which BTP subaccount (and therefore which AI Launchpad subscription) you're using:

https://YOUR-SUBDOMAIN.ai-launchpad.prod.us-east-1.aws.ai-prod.cloud.sap/...

Compare YOUR-SUBDOMAIN against the subdomain in your service key's url field. If they don't match — you're in the wrong Launchpad.

The Fix
  1. Go to BTP Cockpit → the subaccount that contains your AI Core service instance
  2. Navigate to Instances and Subscriptions → SAP AI Launchpad → Open
  3. This opens the Launchpad connected to that specific subaccount's AI Core instance
  4. Create your configuration and deployment here — it will be visible to your service key credentials

Bookmark the AI Launchpad URL for each subaccount separately. When you have multiple subaccounts, it's easy to open the wrong Launchpad instance by navigating directly to the URL without checking which subaccount it belongs to.

QUICK REFERENCE 

Deployment and Configuration Error Quick Reference

 

Error / Symptom

Root Cause

Fix

404 on RUNNING deployment

Token expired or wrong resource group header

Refresh token and confirm AI-Resource-Group header matches deployment's group

404 despite correct token and group

Deployment lives in different AI Core instance than service key

Check instance_id label in resource group — get service key for the correct instance

Resource group stuck in Provisioning

Plan restriction or platform-level issue

Wait 5 min, retry via curl, raise CA-ML-AIP ticket if persists beyond 15 min

409 on resource group delete

Resource group still in active Provisioning state

Wait for PROVISIONED or ERROR status before deleting

Empty response or format error on inference

Calling orchestration deployment with foundation-models format

Check scenarioId in deployment list — use /completion endpoint for orchestration

Deployment in Launchpad but not in curl list

Launchpad connected to wrong subaccount's AI Core instance

Open Launchpad from correct BTP subaccount — check browser URL subdomain

THE WORKING INFERENCE CALL 

Complete Working Model Inference Call

Once all twelve errors are resolved, this is the complete working inference call for a foundation-models deployment. All values in capitals are replaced with your actual values:

# Step 1: Get fresh token (valid 1 hour)
curl -X POST "https://YOUR_SUBDOMAIN.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=YOUR_CLIENTID&client_secret=YOUR_SECRET"
 
# Step 2: List deployments to confirm ID and resource group
curl -X GET "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/lm/deployments" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" ^
  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default"
 
# Step 3: Call the model
curl -X POST "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/inference/deployments/YOUR_DEPLOYMENT_ID/chat/completions" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" ^
  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" ^
  -d "{"model":"YOUR_MODEL_NAME","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello, are you working?"}],"max_tokens":100}"

A successful response contains choices[0].message.content with the model's reply. Your end-to-end connection is proven. From here — move to the generative-ai-hub-sdk for Python integration (covered in the Generative AI Hub series) which handles token management, deployment ID resolution, and model routing automatically.

entry.title: Free migration assessments for modernization with SAP BDC
entry.content:
SAP is currently offering a limited-time complimentary migration assessment service for modernization with SAP Business Data Cloud. All customers running the applications listed below, either on-premises or in a private cloud, are eligible to take advantage of this free service.

  • SAP BW 7.x (all databases) and all versions of BW/4HANA.
  • SAP BusinessObjects (BOBJ), all versions
  • SAP Business Planning and Consolidation (BPC), all versions
  • SAP HANA and SAP IQ databases running custom-built (non-SAP) applications

These assessments provide deep insights into active and inactive objects, offer guidance on what can be modernized, and provide an actionable modernization plan with best practices. 

To better understand the process, diagram below illustrates the process.

Key illustrated steps include

  • Submit an online request, which takes a couple of minutes. SAP will review the requests and approve them on a first-come, first-served basis. Customers will be provided with a few scripts to gather necessary information, which usually takes a few minutes to run on the production systems.
  • Upon sharing with SAP, expert teams will review the information and generate an assessment report.
  • Then, the SAP expert team will deliver a readout session, sharing insights into the system's usage patterns and a personalized transformation journey with modernization options to build a scalable solution and prepare for the era of AI with SAP BDC.

Additionally, the video below walks through the steps to request the free assessment service.

Refer to the links below for additional information and to request free assessments.

  • Assessment Request Form
  • Modernize with SAP Business Data Cloud
  • Evolve your SAP Business Warehouse
  • Readiness assessment service for SAP Business Data Cloud

Join the SAP Data Professional Community for the latest on Data & AI topics.

entry.title: Intermittent Non-Delivery of Emails from “Schedule Billing Output” (F1510)
entry.content:
Good afternoon,

When we attempt to send billing documents directly to customers via a job created in the “Schedule Billing Output” app (F1510), we have noticed that even when the document shows the status “Sent” in the system, customers sometimes do not receive the emails. Is there anything that could be blocking these messages?

Additionally, does the system validate whether the email addresses are valid?

Thank you very much.

entry.title: Creating Production Order Component Line Items via API
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

We are currently working on a Custom Application Developed using the RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) model in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, where Batch wise line items are being managed.

As part of our requirement, we are using the A_ProductionOrderComponent_4 API to update values in the CO02 Production Order screen. However, this API does not fully meet our needs.

At present, when using the PATCH method, we are only able to modify existing line items and are unable to create new ones.

Our requirement is to create new line items in the Production Order Component along with the corresponding batch details. We would like to understand if there is any POST method or appropriate API available to support this functionality. Alternatively, we would appreciate any recommendations for a suitable approach to achieve this requirement.

Thank you,

Production order Component line Item

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: License type for Quality Inspection users (Self-Service vs. Core)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on a proposal for an S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation and I have a specific question regarding the FUE (Full Use Equivalent) consumption for Quality Inspectors.

The scenario is as follows: The users in question only need access to the system to record inspection results (capturing data from a physical inspection into the system). They do not perform any quality planning, usage decisions, or strategic quality management tasks.

Our concern: We are being suggested to use a Core/Professional (CSM Base) license for these users. However, given that their task is purely transactional and repetitive (data entry), we believe this might fall under a Self-Service or Operational user type.

My questions for the experts:

  1. Based on the latest SAP License Guide, does a 'Quality Inspector' role (recording results only) typically trigger a higher FUE count (Core/Professional), or can it be covered by a lower tier?

  2. Which specific Business Catalogs or Fiori Apps for inspection recording are known to stay within the Self-Service/Operational boundaries?

We want to ensure we are proposing the most cost-effective and compliant licensing model for the client. Any insights or documentation references would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,

Sergio Torres

entry.title: What's New in SAP Convergent Charging 2025 FPS1
entry.content:
New Release

PUBLIC

 March 2026

Dear Convergent Charging and BRIM community,

We are glad to announce that, effective March 13th , 2026, the SAP Convergent Charging 2025 FPS1 release is now generally available to our customers.

With this release we have introduced new capabilities that will significantly benefit our customers.  Some of these features have long been requested by our customers, and with this release, we have not only introduced them but also enhanced the user experience and security.

Let's dive into these latest product features in detail highlighting our latest pricing and charging capabilities, as part of the SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM) suite.

Management of the zero value in range tables

Imagine a scenario where you need to calculate commissions on usage revenue. These values are typically maintained in a range table, however, boundary conditions often turn into subtle yet critical challenges. Let’s take an example where your customer’s transactions result in zero net revenue. For instance, when customers fully redeem discount coupons or vouchers, bringing the transaction value down to zero. In such cases, calculating commissions requires support of zero value within the lower bound range definition.

Traditionally, managing this in SAP CC has been restrictive, especially when defining range tables having zero as a range bound value. There has been limited flexibility in configuring whether zero should be treated as inclusive or exclusive, which often made it difficult to accurately model real-world scenarios. As a result, businesses had to rely on workarounds to ensure that zero-value transactions did not incorrectly trigger commissions or pricing outcomes.

Thanks to the new enhancement of Fiori app – ‘Manage Range Table Classes’ where we have introduced a new card to configure inclusion / exclusion of  the zero value for the first range. Now you can explicit configuration of the zero value in range tables, independent of upper bound settings. This gives businesses precise control over edge cases, such as ensuring that zero-value transactions correctly result in zero commissions, in our above example. It simplifies configuration, improves accuracy, and brings pricing logic closer to actual business expectations without the need for complex adjustments.

Figure 1: Management of the zero value in range tables

The Benefits

  • Allows more complex tiered pricing scenario by considering zero-value cases.

 For more information about this new feature, refer to Management of the Lower Bound of the First Range in Range Tables topic on the SAP Help Portal.

Correction of errors and reprocessing of item files

Have your back-office experts or billing specialists ever struggled to resolve failed item files generated during the rating process?  Only to find themselves waiting for admin teams to grant file system access? Most of the times these become an operational bottleneck, slowing down issue resolution and delaying the flow of items into SAP Convergent Invoicing. Recognizing this pain point through customer feedback and real-world use cases, we are happy to introduce a more efficient and user-centric approach.

With the new Resolve Item Loading Issues Fiori app, available in the SAP Convergent Charging Cockpit, billing specialists are now empowered to handle such issues directly, without completely replying on the admin teams. Billing specialist can easily view the erroneous item files and perform activities such as:

  • Trigger rating to resolve issues in item files generated by the rating process having ability to
    • View item files through a worklist with dedicated categorization
    • Download them for local corrections.
    • and either reprocess or delete them as needed.
  • The app also supports bulk reprocessing, allowing multiple item files to be handled in one go, significantly improving efficiency and turnaround time.

In addition to operational flexibility, this enhancement also strengthens governance and transparency. System admins can now track these activities through improved capabilities in the Analyze Item Files and Display User Operations apps, including visibility into deleted item files.

Figure 2: Correction of errors and reprocessing of item files

  • Benefits
    • Eliminates dependency on technical teams or direct file system access.
    • Empowers billing specialists for rating to resolve issues themselves, reducing turnaround time for error correction.

For more information about this new capability, refer to Correction of Errors and Reprocessing of Item Files (New)

Refer to our How-to Video "How to resolve item loading issues" for a glimpse of the new cockpit capability.

SAML SSO for Core Tool

With this release, we have a new SAML-based User Session Authentication feature that brings seamless Single Sign-On (SSO) capabilities to the Core Tool. This allows users to effortlessly access the core tool using sessions that is created by the Cockpit interface. With just a simple one-click button, users can leverage SSO experience, logging in once to gain access without repeatedly entering credentials.

This streamlined approach not only enhances user convenience but also strengthens security by relying exclusively on the trusted SAML authentication protocol. Whether you’re managing multiple SAP tools or looking to simplify your login process, this enhancement ensures smooth, secure access while maintaining strict control over authentication methods.

Figure 3: Single Sign-On Authentication for Core Tool Users

Benefits

  • Allows better user experience
  • Improves security (SEC-230 requirement of Security product standard)

For more information about this new feature, refer to Single Sign-On Authentication for Core Tool Users topic on the SAP Help Portal.

Other Enhancements

In addition to the features mentioned above, SAP Convergent Charging 2025 FPS01 also introduces other enhancements:

New Web services operations for subscriber table management

To further improve integration with SAP CPQ, SAP CC now offers enhanced web services to support the creation and maintenance of subscriber tables.

With the introduction of new web service operations for managing subscriber mapping tables and subscriber range tables during quote creation, data can now be exported and imported in CSV format. In scenarios where the table does not exist, the service will automatically create the mapping or range table, making the integration more robust and better suited for consumption-based scenarios.

Benefits

  • Improves the integration capabilities between SAP CPQ and SAP Convergent Charging regarding subscriber table management

Find more detail about this feature from SAP Help portal:

  • SAP CC 2025 Web Services
    • Subscriber Mapping Table Management
      • Export the data of a subscriber mapping table to CSV
      • Import the data of a subscriber mapping table from CSV
    • Subscriber Range Table Management
      • Export the data of a subscriber range table to CSV
      • Import the data of a subscriber range table from CSV

 

Other enhancements

  • Cockpit capability enhancement with Linear Function component
  • Improved compliance with identity provisioning and lifecycle management for SCIM API
  • Time zone information in Display System Status app
  • Upgrade to SapMachine 25
  • Upgrade to Apache CXF 3.6.9
  • Support for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10
  • Bug fixes: see SAP Note 3660892 - Delivery and Information Note of SAP CC 2025 FPS 1

 

More Information

SAP Convergent Charging 2025 FPS01 has been released to customers on March 13th, 2026,  and is available on the SAP Software Download Center.

For more information on SAP Convergent Charging 2025 FPS 01, check out the following links:

  • What’s New in 2025 FPS01
  • Release Information Note (RIN) for SAP Convergent Charging 2025 FPS1
  • Product Page on SAP Help Portal à Convergent Charging
  • Business AI in BRIM: Joule Help Page     Initial Setup of Joule for BRIM
  • Questions and Answers for SAP Convergent Charging in SAP Community
  • SAP BRIM Customer Influence Portal > Opportunity area “Solution Quotation and BRIM”

entry.title: From Insights to Foresight: Elevating Analytics with SAP Analytics Cloud, Joule, BTP and Perplexity
entry.content:
From Insights to Foresight: Elevating Analytics with
SAP Analytics Cloud, Joule, BTP and Perplexity

Introduction

We’ve all been there: you have the internal sales figures, but you’re missing the "why" from the outside world. Is a raw material shortage in Asia spiking your costs? Is a competitor’s new product launch affecting your pipeline?

In today’s fast-paced business environment, having data isn't enough; you need the right data at the right time, regardless of where it lives. Whether it’s a row in a database, a paragraph in a PDF, or a trending market report on the web, the goal is to provide a unified experience.

This post explores how we are extending the standard capabilities of SAP Business Data Cloud using the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and AI Foundation to create massive value. We will make use of a demo scenario in which our famous BestRunBikes company is facing significant declines in the racing bike segment.

 

1. The Core: Master Your Structured Data with Analytical Insights

The journey begins with making structured data accessible to everyone, not just analysts. With Analytical Insights in Joule (released in Q2 2025), users can interact with their business data using natural language. With Joule in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) we bring this analytical power to the fingertips of our SAC users (planned GA see below).

Example: At the Homescreen of SAC I recognize that my order value faces a significant decrease. We jump into the Metrics list to dive deeper. The Smart Insights (as built-in embedded ai capability) indicates that the racing product type is causing the issue. I want to understand which products belong to that type, so i ask Joule to "show me the product sales for racing" which results in an immediate visual chart generated by SAC.

 

Value:

  • Democratized Access: Analytics are available directly from line-of-business applications, saving time and training costs.
  • Powered by "Just Ask": This feature is enabled by SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and its "just ask" functionality.
  • Deep Integration: It supports both SAC acquired models and SAP Datasphere models, ensuring data-access controls and authorizations remain intact.

Note: The demo scenario shows Joule in SAC, which is a feature that is not yet general available. As of date of the post, the current plan is for Controlled Release with SAC QRC2 2026 & a planned GA with QRC3 2026. The timelines are subject to change as per the usual disclaimers. The experience of Joule itself though can already be achieved with Joule being deployed somewhere else like e.g. Successfactors, S4, Workzone,...

 

2. The Context: Grounding AI in Your Company’s Wisdom

The first step in extending Joule is giving it access to your "dark data"—the unstructured information living in PDFs, images & more. Using the SAP BTP Document Grounding service, we can link Joule to internal sources like SharePoint or Workzone.

Example: In our demo we want to understand if there are certain reasons for the decline in racing bikes. We know that we repeatedly buy market research that lives as pdf in our sharepoint. We understand that there is reduced consumer spending and increased safety concerns from the customers. We could now reach out to the product manager for racing bikes to discuss this.

 

Value:

  • Unstructured Data Integration: Access internal information stored in documents on platforms like SharePoint or Workzone.
  • Internal Knowledge Base: Users can query for internal guidelines, KPI definitions, or existing market research documents.
  • Reliable Foundation: This is an official BTP service, designed to provide "grounding" for AI, ensuring responses are based on your actual business documents.

Have a look at our AI Discovery Center to learn more about this service.

 

3. The Vision: Global Intelligence with Perplexity Integration

The final frontier is connecting your internal world with the external one. By building a custom Joule Skill in Joule Studio, we can tap into the power of Perplexity for real-time web information.

Example: We want to have the latest informations / data around the bike market and specifically look into the european market. We can also ask for certain market projections. With the help of the perplexity skill we can get this kind of information right at the fingertips of the enduser. And now imagine to use this directly as input for simulation & planning!

 

The Architecture Stack

To bring Perplexity into the Joule interface, we utilize three core pillars of the SAP Business Technology Platform:

  • Joule Studio: This is where the Custom Skill is defined and configured. It acts as the front-end logic that tells Joule when to trigger an external search.
  • Cloud Foundry (The CAP Wrapper): We use a Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model wrapper running on Cloud Foundry. This layer handles the API logic, ensuring the data is formatted correctly between the SAP ecosystem and the external provider. Thanks to vibe coding this was minimal effort.
  • AI Foundation (SAP AI Core): The heavy lifting of the AI communication happens here. We use SAP AI Core to securely query Perplexity, providing a robust and governed gateway to the web.

 

Value:

  • External Market Reach: Extend Joule’s information reach to include external market data, projections, and raw material price developments.
  • Technical Synergy: This setup uses SAP AI Core to query Perplexity, acting as a bridge between the enterprise and the live web. It ensures that external queries are handled with enterprise-grade security.
  • Future-Ready: While this can be built today as a custom skill, it is already on the roadmap to become a standard capability of Joule.

 

Conclusion: The Power of an Integrated Platform

By combining the Business Data Cloud (SAC and Datasphere) with the SAP BTP AI Foundation, we aren't just building tools; we are building an intelligent ecosystem. We are turning Joule from an analytical chatbot into a comprehensive business assistant that knows your data, your documents, and the world at large. 

 

Cheers,

Tobias

entry.title: Re: Mandatory Billing from Primary Storage Locations Acro...
entry.content:
Not sure as to why you want the restriction for storage location at billing level which normally happen during delivery creation stage. Is it that always the unrestricted stocks would be placed only in that main storage location as delivery is based on this to post the goods issue.

Nevertheless, implement the rule at billing creation using the Custom Logic app, referencing a table of primary storage locations per plant. This way, deliveries can still happen from alternate locations if stock is short, but billing will be strictly enforced from the designated primary storage location.

But again, there should be proper Business justification for this. If it is based on plant, then ok there is some genuine reason behind this but wondering why storage location.

entry.title: Re: Dangerous goods management: shipping batteries findin...
entry.content:
Hello there is no easy solution possible

Reason: You need a process which is stable in "Sales", "Delivery" and "Transport" Process

The main reason is how the DG set up runs.

You define "DG Master" data. Which is then stable in the process.

I checked these options (only as an idea):

Version 1:  you create a "combined" material (which describes the later "package" to sent)

Pro: You need one Material number (or more); one REAL_SUB you will create  DG master data etc.

Con; You need to train the users, if one customer would like to get your combination of UN numbers; they have to use a special material number

But the full process in Sales, Delivery, and Transport is possible; you are 100% in SAP standard

Version 2:

you ignore the DG Check part and focus on printing (or sending DG data electronically) of DG data on documents. Here you need to add special logic to detemine that both UN Number are going to one package (and if not: keep both UN DG printing)

This would need ABAP development in Delivery, and Transport. (in theory you could extend the DG Check part as well).

This option is only good if you have more than one special case.  If you have only this special case... not sure if this investment is good (depends on how often you are selling both UN numbers in one Package)

Version 3:

You still need one special Material number; but do a direct assignment to DG_CL_SUB object. Perform DG filling etc.

I believe version 1 and 3 are not  bad. Both have pros and cons.

But it is not really foreseen to change DG data in the process

And now we have version 4 (which is not so good): you use the DG template material process. And on Sales Order or better delivery level you "overwrite" the existing DG master data

This is possible in SAP Standard as well; but more complex; more to be trained etc.

PLease check my variants. Wih you the best

C.B.

entry.title: Re: S4 Public Cloud x MBC Setup
entry.content:
Dear @SchauppM,

In Public Cloud the connector for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is configured on the ERP side, meaning in the S/4 Cloud system, not as a separate customizing object that is maintained only in the MBC tenant. SAP Public Cloud onboarding guide explicitly states to configure the connector for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity in your ERP and SAP also documents that the self-service configuration steps for the connector are available in the CBC (Central Business Configuration). The MBC tenant is still relevant, but its role is mainly the service-side connection and administration layer. SAP documents that customers have access to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity applications for ERP configuration and monitoring, and SAP also provides dedicated applications for ERP connections and monitoring in the MBC environment. So the references you see in the guide to ERP connections or MBC applications do not mean that the Public Cloud connector customizing moves from S/4 into the MBC tenant

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/3fa51d2862684bcfa3183bf499792673/bccd65ce454e4068a782e303d30f16d6.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b8e68a90bfff4ff0906d5db5342bdece/1e046db5897144d2be419c393b7d2f80.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/3fa51d2862684bcfa3183bf499792673/38bfe5196742421f8356b7c799e32dfd.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/21222bd7ab6e472cb5e1039c435d4018/09885aadf3fd442fa5bbd7eadbb9e101.html

The point about the connector settings overriding standard configuration should be understood as connector-level processing and routing control, not as a replacement of the underlying S/4 setup. SAP documents connector-specific settings such as processing steps that can override standard processing, sender/receiver ID mappings and outbound routing settings. This means certain technical communication or message-handling parameters can be controlled in the connector layer but the basic S/4 payment and bank communication configuration is still required

Summary:

Maintain the connector-related settings in Public Cloud and use the MBC tenant for onboarding, ERP connection administration and monitoring. In that sense, you are on the right track by making the settings in S/4

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b8e68a90bfff4ff0906d5db5342bdece/27868b27392342afa3d9528df206049f.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b8e68a90bfff4ff0906d5db5342bdece/edff0740c954466389e720b2b0e03ab8.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b8e68a90bfff4ff0906d5db5342bdece/0b9d31db9775491dbb0e3e0f403c7add.htm

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Project Budget not flexible to add budget-based sched...
entry.content:
Hi Customer,

I hope you are well. 

For the Import Financial Plan Data app, the file upload functionality is designed to import financial planning data for existing project elements only.

As a result, the creation of new WBS elements or extension of the project structure via Excel upload is not supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Structural changes must be maintained using the relevant Enterprise Project apps.

For reference, please see:

Import Financial Plan Data

The situation you are referring to does not represent an error in current delivery of S/4HANA Cloud but a new feature request. SAP Support deals with fixing errors in S/4HANA Cloud and does not cover feature requests. 

To see new functionality introduced in the future you are encouraged to create an improvement request. SAP Customer Influence enables customers to submit improvement requests and to openly vote on them. 

You can use the following link to submit your idea, which will then become visible to all other customers who can vote for it in future releases. https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/930 

3206294 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management
https://me.sap.com/notes/3206294/en-US 

Best regards, 

Tiago 
SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Verify General Journal Entries Flexible Workflow, and Extensibility
entry.content:
Journal Entry Upload Requirements

Do you have a custom solution for uploading journal entries with workflow approvals and email notifications? Are you converting from ECC to S/4HANA and would like to bring your custom code back to clean core? Is Fiori part of UX Journey? If you answered yes to any of those, then this blog post is for you.

Custom Code Solutions

Before the advent of Fiori apps, SAP customers had to create their own custom code to manage the uploading of journal entries via Excel. I have worked with customers that have different solution design when it comes to journal upload. They come in various shapes and sizes like LSMW, custom programs, and even third-party tools. Those were the days when functional consultants had to be creative and work together with technical consultants to address the business requirements around journal entries upload and approvals.

Fiori Apps and Extensibility towards Clean Core

In SAP S/4HANA, we now have a set of standard Fiori Apps that can address the same business requirements and maintain a clean core. This blog post (based on SAP S/4HANA 2023) is a result of different customer projects with varying business requirements. It ranges from a simple (greenfield) to highly complex (system conversion) custom built solutions. 

The information presented intends to equip the functional and technical consultants with Fiori Apps and available extensibility when it comes to journal entries. Here is the list of Fiori Apps that needs to be activated and deployed depending on business requirements.

Fiori App IDFiori App NameDescription Tips and Tricks
F2547AVerify General Journal Entries - For RequesterAs a requester (for example, an accountant), with the Verify General Journal Entries - For Requester app, you can create, display and edit a journal entry, and then submit it for verification. In addition, you could upload the general journal entries via EXCEL or CSV file and then submit multiple journal entries for verification. For the rejected ones, you could resubmit for verification. Afterwards, you can track status and whole change history for workflow.This app can be used as the single point of entry for users to upload a journal entry.
F2548

Upload General Journal Entries

With this app, you can upload multiple general journal entries from a spreadsheet or a CSV file. The template for uploading is available in this app. After you successfully finish the upload, you can post the general journal entries directly. Postings can be made in all relevant ledgers for the accounts for which you are responsible. If not all the journal entries in a file are uploaded successfully, you must correct errors, copy the batch ID of the initial upload to the file, and then repeat upload.

You can navigate to this Fiori App from F2547A via the Upload button.

If you need to enforce that attachments should be mandatory, you can hide the Submit button and users have to go inside the uploaded journal entry to upload attachments. The Submit button will only be clicked after attaching the supporting documentation. An error message could be raised if the attachment is not greater than zero.

F2720

Manage Workflows for Journal Entry Verification - In General Ledger

Before you start to verify general journal entries or currency adjustments, you can define a workflow for your business needs and then activate it with this Manage Workflows for Journal Entry Verification - In General Ledger app. All journal entries and currency adjustments that need to be verified go through the workflow when they meet the preconditions that you define in this app to start a workflow.

Depending on your business requirements, you can determine the approver (e.g. based on HR position) without using the Manage Teams and Responsibilities for General Journal Entry Verification. Please refer to SAP note 3603584 - How to extend Verify General Journal Entries with Custom Rule - SAP for Me on the available extensibility. The step-by-step guide can be found in the attachment of this note.

F3932

Manage Teams and Responsibilities for Journal Entry Verification - In General Ledger

With this feature you can:

  • View the hierarchical structure of teams, sub teams, and super teams for these team categories: FGJEV
Maintain values for custom responsibility definitions in a team

If the requester and approver can be managed using this Fiori app, then it should suffice bearing in mind that it might have some limitations and scalability considerations. The extensibility for custom rule can be considered if necessary.

F6957

Custom Logic

With this app, you can implement BAdIs that have been released by SAP. You can start implementing from the Custom Logic app or from the UI adaptation in an application that can be extended.This is where the extensibility can address the determination of approvers via BAdI.
F4637

Manage Responsibility Rules

With the Manage Responsibility Rules application, you can see a list of standard responsibility rules delivered by SAP application teams. These rules are used to determine agents that are responsible for workflows and situation handling.Familiarise yourself with the concept of CDS views.
F4636

Manage Responsibility Contexts

With the Manage Responsibility Contexts application, you can use the list of standard responsibility contexts delivered by SAP application teams for agent determination.

You can also extend a standard responsibility context by adding your custom agent rule to determine agents that are responsible for your business process tasks, objects, and situation handling.

Use this in conjunction with Manage Responsibility Rules

F1306

Maintain Email Templates

Using this app, you can define email templates to streamline your email correspondence. Templates consist of fixed content (such as subject or disclaimer) and variable parts (such as the form of address).

You can put a link in the email notification so that it can be directed to the My Inbox Fiori app. The URL for your different environments can be maintained as variables.

Consider whether it is really necessary to send email notifications (nobody likes a flooded mailbox) or the in-app Fiori notification will be sufficient.

F1590

Configure Software Packages

With this app, you can configure software packages for transporting extension items.

This is necessary for any development.

F1589

Register Extensions for Transport

With this app you, can register extensions for being transported in your production system.

A transport request is still required but this Fiori Apps is necessary for extensions made via Fiori Apps.

The links provided in this blog was at the time that SAP S/4HANA 2025 is already available. Always review the latest features based on your SAP S/4HANA versions. I hope this blog has helped you together with the other available articles in our community.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Performance (Forms): Option to disable auto-collapsing of section description
entry.content:
Currently, in the Latest Version of Performance Management, in Forms, section descriptions are automatically collapsed under a "Show more" button. This behavior is hardcoded and cannot be changed by administrators.

Business Need / Impact: While the "cleaner" look might be intended for mobile devices, it creates significant friction for users working on desktops.

  • Users have to perform hundreds of extra clicks per cycle just to see the instructions or descriptions for each section.

  • Constant clicking "Show more" breaks the flow of the performance review.

  • Crucial instructions are often missed because they are hidden by default, leading to lower data quality in the forms.

Proposed Solution: We request a new setting in Form Template Settings or Display Settings that allows administrators to choose the default state of section descriptions.

  • Option A: Expand all section descriptions by default.

  • Option B: Keep the "Show more" button (current state).

Having an "Expand All by Default" option would significantly improve the user experience and save valuable time during the peak of the Performance and Talent Management cycles.

I am attaching a screenshot from our system to illustrate the current issue:

entry.title: Re: API for : Manage Budget Documents - Project app
entry.content:
Hi Customer,

I hope you are well. 

Currently, the functionality available in the Fiori app "Manage Budget Documents - Project" is not exposed via any released public API. Budget document processing is part of the internal project control framework and involves validations such as availability control, version management, and audit tracking. For this reason, SAP does not provide an external API to create or update project budgets.

If automation is a critical requirement, we recommend raising an enhancement request via the SAP Customer Influence portal.

To see new functionality introduced in the future you are encouraged to create an improvement request. SAP Customer Influence enables customers to submit improvement requests and to openly vote on them. 

You can use the following link to submit your idea, which will then become visible to all other customers who can vote for it in future releases. https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/930 

3206294 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management
https://me.sap.com/notes/3206294/en-US 

Have a great day!  

Best regards, 

Tiago 
SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Re: Mandatory Billing from Primary Storage Locations Acro...
entry.content:
Dear @tushar_sonawane53,

In Public Cloud this requirement is not controlled in billing itself. Since billing is created from billing-relevant SD documents such as outbound deliveries the correct control point is outbound delivery, picking and goods issue rather than billing configuration. The standard approach is to configure automatic determination of the primary picking storage location of each plant as the automatically determined picking storage location. SAP determines the picking storage location based on shipping point, plant and storage condition. For 27 plants this means maintaining the main storage location of each plant in the delivery determination logic so that the delivery is created with the correct storage location by default

Additional Information: SAP also supports storage location substitution in outbound processing. Therefore, if the business requirement is that billing must only follow goods issued from the designated primary storage location the process should be designed without substitution to alternative storage locations

If the requirement is strict and no exception should be allowed this should be treated as a delivery-processing control, not as a billing setting. In that case the standard determination logic should be assessed via released Delivery Management extensibility to validate or block non-primary storage locations before the downstream billing step

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/7dc3125477a98c24e10000000a4450e5.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/aff0b3f5f46c42a2b2c0fabfc233bab2/ebf065549bd445c59794fb8126d8c46b.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/cd6af9797ce84fedad19cb896ef51b1f.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/aff0b3f5f46c42a2b2c0fabfc233bab2/78ac6e6632494a8dbc0a63c8b63f6d77.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: HOW TO UNSUBSCRIBE FROM ARIBA NOTIFICATIONS AND SAP EMAIL LISTS
entry.content:
In this blog we will explain how to send an unsubscribe request for Ariba Notifications and also how to unsubscribe from SAP email lists.

There are different ways to Unsubscribe from Ariba Notifications depending on the source of the email that you are getting. 

Please find below the different options: 

  1. If you unsubscribe request is for email notifications from "SAP Business Network" or "SAP Ariba" please follow the steps below: 
  • Login to SAP Ariba Supplier's page (the user will be the email address that is getting the notifications and If you don't remember the password, you will need to submit a password reset) 
  • Go to your initial's (top right corner of the page) 
  • Click settings 
  • Click notifications in their account and disable all the notification options.  

If you’re no longer using the account, this can be deleted, however you would need to contact the Ariba Support Team. 

All account removal requests concerning Ariba/ SAP Business Network fall only under the scope of Ariba privacy team: privacy.ariba@sap.com  

2. For non-customers receiving the emails from another source and there is no “unsubscribe” option, then we would advise you to use the following link:  

https://www.sap.com/profile/unsubscribe.html 

Thank you for reading!   

We hope you found this helpful.  

Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have!  

You can contact CIC for assistance if you need any further guidance. The Customer Interaction Center can be contacted by Phone, Expert Chat and by email.  

Don't Miss Out on Free Webcasts  

We would like to invite you to our free webcasts, which we believe can help you get the best from your support in SAP. You can find all upcoming CIC Webcasts. These sessions are open to all the users and will revolve around hot topics in support today such as SAP for Me, Case Management, etc.  

Additionally, you can explore a range of CIC how-to videos at learning.sap.com. Please feel free to share this information with your colleagues. We are looking forward to your attendance.  

Lastly, you may want to access all CIC Blogs here:  https://community.sap.com/t5/tag/CustomerInteractionCenter/tg-p/board-id/crm-blog-sap.

entry.title: VIM 7.5 ECC – MRKO Posting Does Not Finalize DP Workflow (BUS_OBJTYPE / BUS_OBJKEY Missing)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m currently working on an issue in SAP ECC with OpenText VIM 7.5, related to MRKO posting and VIM Document Processing (DP) workflow finalization, and I’m looking for guidance or best practices from anyone who has faced a similar scenario.

System Context

  • SAP ECC
  • OpenText VIM 7.5
  • Invoice processing uses VIM DP with workflow
  • Posting is done via MRKO (consignment settlement) rather than MIRO

Current Design

  • The VIM workflow starts correctly.
  • The posting step uses a custom copy of /OPT/VIM_52_BAPI_DOCLINES_PO.
  • Inside this copy, MRKO is executed via SUBMIT.
  • MRKO successfully creates the FI document (BELNR / BUKRS / GJAHR).

Issue / Symptoms

After the FI document is created:

  • The workflow remains active in SWIA and is not completed.
  • VIM Analytics loses the workflow association.
  • In the workflow container, BUS_OBJTYPE and BUS_OBJKEY are empty.

Root Cause (Analysis So Far)

From our investigation, the problem does not seem to be MRKO itself, but how it is executed:

  • MRKO is triggered outside of VIM’s standard DP posting framework.
  • As a result, DP is never finalized.
  • VIM is not informed of the created FI document.
  • BOR linkage is missing, so the workflow cannot bind to the posted document and close properly.

Has anyone come across this situation when using MRKO with VIM DP? What is the recommended or supported way to correctly finalize DP and close the workflow in this scenario? Are there specific VIM classes or methods that should be used to link the FI document back to the DP document? Any advice or experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Designing Goods Receipt Strategies in SAP S/4HANA:An End-to-End Analysis of 101 and 107/109 Move
entry.content:
Hi @mauriciocastiblanco 

My updates are as follows:

#1: Purchase order creation: In this document, are the plant and storage location entered EWM?

Reply: Yes

#2#3: Executed 107 movement: Is this goods receipt posting to an EWM plant/storage location or a non-EWM storage location?
When executing movement 107, in which storage location is the stock temporarily stored?

Reply: 107 movement only have the plant, which says material is in transit. It does not include storage location. after 109 only, physical material will be received at storage location level.

Please let me know, if you have any question futrther.

entry.title: Delivery partner details from CDS not released for development
entry.content:
The CDS views to fetch the delivery partner details such as I_DeliveryDocumentPartner, I_DeliveryPartnerAddress, I_OutboundDeliveryPartnerTP are not released to use for development.

Please provide any accessible CDS views for the same.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Delivery partner details from CDS not released for de...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your inquiry regarding the use of CDS views for retrieving delivery partner details.

We would like to inform you that the CDS views I_DeliveryDocumentPartner, I_DeliveryPartnerAddress, and I_OutboundDeliveryPartnerTP are currently not released for use in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition extensibility scenarios.

As per SAP’s extensibility guidelines, only released CDS views and APIs can be used for custom development. Unreleased objects are restricted and may change without notice.

We recommended the following approach to achieve your requirement using supported artifacts, see options below:

Option 1 – Using Released CDS Views

You can derive delivery partner information by combining:

I_DeliveryDocument (to obtain partner roles such as Ship-To / Sold-To)
I_BusinessPartner
I_BusPartAddress
I_Address_2

This approach allows you to retrieve business partner and address details in a supported manner.

Option 2 – Using Official API (Recommended for Partner Data)

Alternatively, you can use the released API:

Service: API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV
Entity: A_OutbDeliveryPartner

This API provides direct access to outbound delivery partner data and is the closest supported alternative to the unreleased CDS views.

Important nota that currently, there is no single released CDS view that fully replaces the functionality of the unreleased delivery partner CDS views mentioned above. Therefore, a combination of released CDS views or the use of the official API is required.

We hope this clarifies the situation. 

In case this CDS views are critical for your business, you are encouraged to create an improvement request. SAP Customer Influence enables customers to submit improvement requests and to openly vote on them.

3206294 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management
https://me.sap.com/notes/3206294/en-US 

Regards,
Leticia
SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Why SAP Databricks Genie Is a Game‑Changer: Key Benefits and Expert Tips
entry.content:
SAP Databricks Genie Code is an AI-powered assistant inside Databricks that helps you generate, optimize, explain, and fix code or queries directly in notebooks, SQL editors, dashboards, and jobs. It works both as a conversational helper and in “Agent mode” where it can autonomously handle multi-step tasks.

To open Genie Code pane, click on the upper-right corner of the page. 

How Genie Code Works

  • Context-aware assistant: Genie Code understands the environment you’re working in (SQL editor, notebook, file editor, etc.) and tailors its suggestions accordingly.
  • Conversational interface: You can ask questions in plain English, and Genie Code translates them into SQL or Python queries.
  • Agent mode: Beyond single queries, Genie Code can autonomously execute multi-step workflows, such as preparing data, running transformations, and generating reports.
  • Integration with Databricks-hosted AI models: By default, Genie Code uses Databricks’ own models, but you can enable partner-powered AI features for extended capabilities

Key Benefits

  • No-code analytics: Genie Code allows business users to query data without writing SQL, making Databricks more accessible.
  • Faster development: Developers can generate boilerplate code, optimize queries, and debug issues quickly.
  • Self-service BI: Teams can ask natural language questions and instantly get insights, charts, or summaries.
  • Error reduction: Genie Code explains code snippets, helping users understand logic and avoid mistakes.

Quick Comparison: Genie Code vs. Manual Coding

Feature 
Genie Code Manual Coding
Query generationNatural language → SQL/PythonRequires SQL/Python expertise
Error fixingAutomated suggestionsManual debugging
Workflow automationAgent mode handles multi-step tasksMust script everything
AccessibilityBusiness users can query dataLimited to technical users
SpeedInstant insightsSlower, depends on coding skill

Type questions or slash commands  in the text box at the bottom of Genie Code pane and press Enter or click

 Short Demo of SAP Incentive Management - Agent Mode for TCMP Table for CS_Payments table Integration

How Genie Code Works with MCP

  • Agent Mode only: MCP servers are supported exclusively when Genie Code runs in Agent mode.
  • Workspace integration: Genie Code can use any MCP servers added to your Databricks workspace, provided you have permission.
  • Tool exposure: MCP servers expose tools, datasets, and workflows to Genie Code, enabling it to execute tasks like:
    • Running ETL pipelines
    • Querying external APIs
    • Automating reporting workflows
  • Dynamic context retrieval: Instead of hardcoding instructions, Genie Code fetches relevant context from MCP servers in real time.

Genie Code pane controls and settings for MCP, Instructions and Skills

Databricks Genie integrates with MCP (Model Context Protocol) to connect AI agents with external tools, workflows, and contextual data. This allows Genie Code to go beyond simple query generation and act as a true agent that can access structured resources in your workspace.

entry.title: Error Message LB261 - Material Master Data Error (?) Message
entry.content:
Hi all ,

we are doing an upgrade from version 1909 to 2023 .
During our test we are facing a strange error (?) in the system.

In MM02 for some materials we have two different and alternative unit of measure (MASS - KG and  G) because we are manaing a conversion between KG and Meters in Proport. Unit of Measure

During an update for this material i'm facing this error and system don't give me the possibility to do anything else:

We need the conversion and the units of measure in the tab. Alternative unit of measure for business requirements.
Could you face already this error? How can we manage it?
Thanks

entry.title: 🇧🇷 ABAP AI: Capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers CodeJam (Campinas, Brazil)
entry.content:
In this CodeJam session, you will learn how to use ABAP capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers, including predictive code completion, unit test generation for ABAP CDS and ABAP code, and code explanation to speed up your development with ABAP Cloud.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Saturday 11th April 2026 in Campinas, Brazil from 13:15 - 17:15 local time. The language and content of the event will be in English.

Location:

Event Address: FACAMP Faculdades de Campinas - Avenida Alan Turing - Cidade Universitária, Campinas - SP, Brasil

** IMPORTANT ** Only participants of the SAP INSIDE TRACK CAMPINAS who are registered HERE are allowed to attend!

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you wish to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right.

The instructors for this event are the Developer Advocates @thomas_jung and @RichHeilman 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Campinas, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use embeddings to improve your RAG response and build your own chatbot with memory.

Prerequisites: Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Saturday 11th April 2026 in Campinas, Brazil from 13:15 - 17:15 local time. The language and content of the event will be in English.

Location:

Event Address: FACAMP Faculdades de Campinas - Avenida Alan Turing - Cidade Universitária, Campinas - SP, Brasil

** IMPORTANT ** Only participants of the SAP INSIDE TRACK CAMPINAS who are registered HERE are allowed to attend!

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you wish to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right.

The instructors for this event are the Developer Advocates @Vitaliy-R and @noravonthenen

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
Challenge completed

Package: ZAC_46923

After adding a check to avoid an endless save loop it works fine 🙂
I did have to instruct Joule exactly how to write the logic because it wanted to use syntax (class methods) that do not exist.
It was a minus point that Joule is not aware of what standard classes and methods actually exist.

Still, In the end the code is there and it is working, while still allowing the user to overrule the determination.
